nodes.label: Central Current Students Hub
nodes.description: students.unimelb.edu.au — the university's primary current-students web portal, covering transactional admin (enrolment, fees, exams, graduation, key dates, timetable), general support services (health, counselling, Stop 1, careers, student life), and topic-level guidance. Does NOT cover course plans, placements/WIL, or discipline-specific forms.

nodes.label: UniMelb Current Students IA Audit Project
nodes.description: An evidence-based audit of fragmentation across the central hub and 9 faculty 'Current Students' sections, producing a crawled inventory, service-model comparison, and unification strategy delivered as data, a PDF report, and an executive deck.

nodes.label: Course Planning
nodes.description: Degree-specific course plans, sample plans, subject selection guidance, and My Course Planner tool references. Every faculty (9/9) plus schools (MBS, Biomedical) provide this — the most universally faculty-owned topic. 207 pages captured; MBS + Biomedical account for 46%. Legitimate faculty content but thin hub-overlapping layer of generic advice.

nodes.label: Enrolment & Course Admin
nodes.description: Enrolment, re-enrolment, accept-offer, subject enrolment, course transfers. The hub is the definitive source; 4 of 9 faculties duplicate (Law, FEIT, Arts, Education). 84 pages captured, concentrated in Law (25 pages).

nodes.label: Exams, Assessment & Results
nodes.description: Exams, assessment policies, results, rankings, special consideration. Hub is the primary source; some faculties (FEIT, Education, Law) provide parallel content. 78 pages captured.

nodes.label: Fees & Finance
nodes.description: Tuition fees, payment, census dates, scholarships. Hub-owned; 33 pages captured with faculty content being mostly course-plan or scholarship overlap rather than standalone fee information.

nodes.label: Graduation
nodes.description: Graduation ceremonies, apply-to-graduate, awards, honours. The hub owns the apply-to-graduate spine but 125 pages captured are mostly HDR candidature lifecycle + honours/awards — mislabelled topic. Almost no actual ceremony content.

nodes.label: Careers & Employability
nodes.description: The most duplicated service across the university. Every faculty (8/9) and MBS run career content (mentoring, internships, career pathways) in parallel with the central careers hub. 141 pages captured. Hub and faculty career content do not cross-link.

nodes.label: Placements & Work-Integrated Learning
nodes.description: Clinical placements, teaching practicums, legal clinics, engineering internships. MDHS holds 47% of 206 pages (clinical compliance). Near-zero hub overlap — the gap is discoverability (hub has no WIL front door), not duplication. Genuinely faculty-owned.

nodes.label: Student Life & Clubs
nodes.description: Faculty-specific clubs, societies, events, newsletters, enrichment activities, mentoring programs, awards. Largely legitimate faculty-owned content. 214 pages captured but ~⅓ is internal bloat (MSD studio archives, Science committee rosters).

nodes.label: Academic Skills
nodes.description: Discipline-specific academic skills support: legal research, academic English, thesis writing, lab skills. Only 37 pages captured — the smallest topic. Hub has general PASS/academic skills but doesn't cover legal writing, lab reports, studio practice.

nodes.label: Health & Wellbeing
nodes.description: Student health, counselling, wellbeing resources. Hub is the primary source through Counselling & Psychological Services. 6 of 9 faculties also provide wellbeing pages. 87 pages — half is MDHS clinical-placement compliance mis-tagged; genuine pastoral content is 6 near-duplicate 'Wellbeing + Ambassadors' landings.

nodes.label: Scholarships, Prizes & Awards
nodes.description: Faculty-specific scholarship and prize pages, mostly generic process scaffolding (rounds, T&Cs, FAQs, winner galleries). 141 pages; FEIT holds 50%. Hub holds zero — recommendation is to consolidate into central scholarships.unimelb.edu.au.

nodes.label: International Student Support & Study Overseas
nodes.description: Mislabelled topic: ~80% is faculty-owned outbound mobility (exchange, degree partnerships), especially Law (55 pages of exchange partner detail). Genuine inbound support (visas/OSHC/ESOS) is absent from the crawl and hub-owned.

nodes.label: IT & Systems
nodes.description: Artefact-tagged topic: 78% is mis-classified FEIT content (183 of 235 pages). Genuine ~30–40 pages cover physical labs, equipment loans (faculty-owned), and Student IT / LMS / portal spine (hub-owned).

nodes.label: Forms & Administration
nodes.description: Faculty-specific forms: placement applications, ensemble registration, special permission, clinic enrolment. Concentrated in Law (66 pages) + MDHS (70 pre-placement compliance). 212 pages. Faculty forms hubs are legitimate; offer-acceptance/enrolment/special-con admin overlaps the hub.

nodes.label: Contacts & Support
nodes.description: Most diffuse topic. 177 pages — ~half is Arts+Law restatements of hub wellbeing/equity/careers plus duplicate/stub pages. Includes student advisory councils, milestone reviews, career mentoring, peer mentoring.

nodes.label: Inclusion & Equity
nodes.description: Noise-heavy topic: ~45% false positives from MSD studio archives and Law GRD researcher profiles. Genuine ~13 pages of Indigenous/equity/disability content. 49 pages total. Recommendation: consolidate; parallel-reinvents the central service.

nodes.label: Research Candidature (HDR)
nodes.description: Legitimate faculty-owned content: milestones, studios, faculty funding. Inflated by ~70 Law researcher-profile pages. 241 pages. Hub-own the candidature lifecycle spine; convert bios to a directory.

nodes.label: Orientation
nodes.description: Inflated topic: FEIT holds 204 of 289 pages (71%) by nesting its whole estate under /orientation/ paths. Genuine orientation is a thin faculty-owned welcome layer. Second-largest topic by page count.

nodes.label: Subjects & Timetable
nodes.description: Largest topic at 319 pages, but 64% is ABP/MSD's design-studio archive. Remainder is a thin hub-owned timetabling spine (MyTimetable, census, overloading) echoed by faculties plus legitimate discipline subject catalogues.

nodes.label: Transactional Administration
nodes.description: Enrolment + exams/results + special consideration + fees + library. Only 6 of 227 pages sit on the hub domain; faculty content is restatement/local-wrappers/noise. The estate's clearest consolidation target — the hub already owns this.

nodes.label: Clubs & Events
nodes.description: Overwhelmingly faculty community content. 104 pages. Only hub overlap is the generic club-admin wrapper plus self-duplication. FEIT has the most (34 pages). Faculty-owned.

nodes.label: Library
nodes.description: Smallest topic at only 7 pages. Mostly Arts and Law content about research support and facilities. Minimal faculty coverage.

nodes.label: Faculty of Arts
nodes.description: CS at arts.unimelb.edu.au/students. 91 pages, ~80K words, max depth 5. Well-structured local hub with genuine faculty content + moderate hub links. Schools route to faculty CS with some deep-linking. Has language placement tests, Diploma in Languages, Arts Career Mentoring, Melbourne Peer Mentor Program, experiential learning.

nodes.label: Faculty of Engineering & IT
nodes.description: CS at eng.unimelb.edu.au/students. 204 pages, 156K words, max depth 6. Almost entirely self-contained — largest and deepest faculty site. Runs parallel systems for course planning, careers, scholarships, forms, academic skills. Has internship program, hackathon festival, clubs, technical skills series, global challenges, peer connect. 17 broken/redirect URLs. Path-nesting inflates orientation and IT-systems topic counts.

nodes.label: Melbourne Law School
nodes.description: CS at law.unimelb.edu.au/students. 195 pages, ~150K words, max depth 4. True sub-tree: /students/jd, /students/masters, /students/graduate-research. Almost entirely self-contained. MLS Career Services, steps to practising law, degree partnerships with global universities, legal clinics, Ian Malkin Centre for Legal Academic Skills. Has ~70 researcher-profile pages that inflate topic counts.

nodes.label: Faculty of Science
nodes.description: CS at science.unimelb.edu.au/students. 94 pages, ~90K words, max depth 3. Single landing page with in-page anchors (course guides UG/PG, studying & student life, news, support) + per-degree course guide pages. Schools route to faculty CS. Has Science Academic English Tutorials, Career Enrichment Program, internship subjects.

nodes.label: ABP / Melbourne School of Design
nodes.description: CS at msd.unimelb.edu.au/current-students. ~40 pages, ~40K words, max depth ~3. Hub-dependent link directory with some unique content. Has IT Support, clubs info, list of forms, MSD studio archives (~700 near-identical past-studio pages inflate topic counts). ABP Industry Mentoring, Internships & Vocational Placements.

nodes.label: Faculty of Business & Economics
nodes.description: CS at fbe.unimelb.edu.au/students. ~25 pages, ~20K words, max depth ~2. High hub dependency, mostly a link directory. Has student computing spaces, virtual lab, compulsory quantitative requirement, BCom Career Launchpad. /current-students → 404 trap. Department pages route to central hub.

nodes.label: Faculty of Education
nodes.description: CS at education.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-students (not /students or /current-students — both 404). ~40 pages, ~40K words, max depth ~3. Mixed: unique program content + heavy hub-redirect for transactions. Task pages: timetable, Academic Skills module, LANTITE, ICAS, ITEA, enrolment/re-enrolment, Career Mentoring, Master of Teaching placement info.

nodes.label: Faculty of Fine Arts & Music
nodes.description: CS at finearts-music.unimelb.edu.au/current-students. ~40 pages, ~30K words, max depth ~3. Completely self-contained — conservatorium/art-school model doesn't fit hub taxonomy. No sidebar navigation, cards/grid pattern. Has tech support & facilities, instrument loans, research ethics, student services. Wilin Centre keeps own Indigenous student-support page.

nodes.label: Faculty of Medicine, Dentistry & Health Sciences
nodes.description: CS at mdhs.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-students. ~50 pages (with schools), ~50K words, max depth ~3. Card-based landing page, thin hub (4 cards). Most fragmented faculty: 3+ schools run own CS sections on separate subdomains. Content: fitness-to-practice, student enrichment & support, placements/WIL. Schools route to central hub, NOT faculty CS — creating a split experience.

nodes.label: Melbourne Business School
nodes.description: Separate CS at mbs.unimelb.edu.au/students — separate from FBE faculty. Has its own branded career service (mbs.unimelb.edu.au/career), extensive course plans for ~30 masters programs (58 pages of course-planning), computing spaces, virtual lab. Breaks the faculty model.

nodes.label: School of Biomedical Sciences
nodes.description: CS at biomedicalsciences.unimelb.edu.au/study/current-student-information — separate from MDHS faculty. Has its own distinct template. Content: academic skills for BBiomed and MBiomedSci, 16 redirect stubs, student portal (SSO-gated). Bachelor of Science link points circularly back to same page.

nodes.label: Melbourne Dental School
nodes.description: CS at dental.unimelb.edu.au/study/student-resources — separate from MDHS faculty. Has its own CS section, further fragmenting the MDHS student experience.

nodes.label: Melbourne School of Population & Global Health
nodes.description: Likely has its own CS section at mspgh.unimelb.edu.au, further adding to MDHS fragmentation. Not fully confirmed in crawl.

nodes.label: Four URL Conventions for CS Sections
nodes.description: The single concept 'information for current students' is served at four different URL patterns: /students (Law, Arts, FEIT, Science, FBE), /current-students (ABP/MSD, FFAM), /study/current-students (Education, MDHS), and school-level variants. No discoverability by convention — a student cannot guess their faculty's CS URL.

nodes.label: Fragmentation Spectrum
nodes.description: Faculties fall on a spectrum from hub-dependent link directories (ABP, FBE) to self-contained parallel ecosystems (FEIT, Law, FFAM). No consistent model exists for what a faculty CS section should contain.

nodes.label: School-Level CS Fragmentation
nodes.description: MDHS has the worst fragmentation: 3+ schools (Biomedical Sciences, Dental School, MSPGH) run their own CS sections on separate subdomains — breaking the faculty model. FBE has a similar split with MBS. No other faculty has school-level CS sections. A Biomedicine student potentially navigates 3 different CS experiences.

nodes.label: Careers & Employability Duplication
nodes.description: The most duplicated service: every faculty (8/9) and MBS run career content in parallel with the central careers hub. Hub careers content and faculty career content do not cross-link. A student using the hub career checklist may never discover their faculty's mentoring program, and vice versa.

nodes.label: Course Planning Duplication
nodes.description: Every faculty provides course plans. Legitimate: degree-specific sample plans, program-specific requirements, subject selection for specific majors. Duplicative: general 'how to plan your course' advice, My Course Planner links from multiple faculties, leave of absence/course withdrawal info (hub has definitive pages).

nodes.label: Broken and Circular Links
nodes.description: FBE /current-students → 404. FEIT has 17 broken/redirect URLs. Arts has 3 broken pages. Biomedical Sciences has circular link on Bachelor of Science page. MDHS school resource links point back to same thin hub page.

nodes.label: Central Hub Content Gaps
nodes.description: Hub has no content for: course plans/degree-specific planning, placements & WIL, discipline-specific academic skills, faculty-specific forms, professional recognition/accreditation pathways. These exist only on faculty sites.

nodes.label: No 'What's Where' Guidance
nodes.description: No faculty CS page tells students 'for X, go to the central hub; for Y, stay here.' Students must learn through trial and error which system handles which need. No page maps 'I need to do X → go to Y.'

nodes.label: CMS / Platform Inconsistency
nodes.description: Law, Arts, FEIT, Science appear to use the same Squiz Matrix CMS with consistent header/footer. FFAM uses a different template (no sidebar, cards/grid). MDHS uses card-based pattern. FBE and MBS use different visual treatments despite being under same faculty. Biomedical Sciences has its own distinct template.

nodes.label: URL Convention Dead-End Traps
nodes.description: Education and MDHS both 404 on /students and /current-students — their real CS sections are only at /study/current-students. A student (or staffer) guessing the 'usual' URL hits a Page Not Found.

nodes.label: Dual-Path Fragmentation
nodes.description: The shared global UoM header carries a 'Students' link pointing to students.unimelb.edu.au on every unit site — so the central hub is always one click away. Yet faculties simultaneously maintain parallel local CS sections. This dual-path is the core of the fragmented experience.

nodes.label: Topic Classifier Data-Quality Artifacts
nodes.description: Four systematic inflations: (1) MSD studio archive (~700 near-identical past-studio pages) inflates subjects-timetable and student-life; (2) FEIT path-nesting inflates orientation and it-systems; (3) Law researcher-profile pages (~70 GRD bios) inflate research-candidature and inclusion-equity; (4) tag mislabelling — international is mostly outbound mobility, graduation is mostly HDR lifecycle. Topic counts overstate real content.

nodes.label: Consolidate Transactional Admin to Hub
nodes.description: Enrolment, fees, exams, graduation, special consideration are already hub-owned. Faculty restatements should be replaced with links to the hub. The estate's clearest consolidation target — only 6 of 227 pages sit on the hub domain.

nodes.label: Create WIL/Placements Discovery Hub
nodes.description: Placements/WIL is completely absent from the central hub. Students must find placement info on faculty sites. The hub should add a WIL front door that routes students to their faculty placement pages — fixing the discoverability gap without centralizing the faculty-owned content.

nodes.label: Standardize Faculty CS IA
nodes.description: All faculty CS sections should use a consistent URL pattern and information architecture template. A shared CS template with standard sections plus faculty-specific slots would eliminate the 4-URL-convention problem and CMS inconsistency.

nodes.label: Cross-Link Hub and Faculty Career Content
nodes.description: Hub careers content and faculty career content must cross-link. A student using the hub career checklist should discover their faculty's mentoring program, and vice versa. Does not require content migration — just bidirectional links.

nodes.label: Fix Classifier Data-Quality Artifacts
nodes.description: De-archive MSD studios from the crawl; flatten FEIT path-nesting and URL-churn (17 broken/redirect URLs); convert ~70 Law researcher bios to a directory; fix Biomedical's 16 redirect stubs. These artifacts inflate topic counts and distort analysis.

nodes.label: Create 'What's Where' Service Map
nodes.description: Create a page (on hub or faculty sites) that maps 'I need to do X → go to Y' for every student need — clarifying which services live on the hub vs faculty sites. Addresses the fundamental discoverability problem.

nodes.label: Service-Model Matrix
nodes.description: A cross-faculty matrix classifying each of 19 service categories as Hub-provided (H), Faculty-provided (F), Both (B — potential duplication), Not observed (—), or School-level (S). Based on crawl of 9 faculty CS sections + central hub (584 fully crawled pages + ~200 IA-captured).

nodes.label: Claude-in-Chrome Sequential Crawl
nodes.description: All pages fetched through Claude-in-Chrome (user's connected browser) because WebFetch is WAF-blocked (403) and Apify is over monthly limit. Crawling is sequential, resumable across sessions, using BFS per unit. Writes local corpus to crawl/ directory; analysis reads files, never the live site. Public pages only; auth-gated content recorded as gateway.

nodes.label: Page Classification Taxonomy
nodes.description: Four-class taxonomy applied at crawl time: link-farm/hub (mostly outbound links → hub, ≤150 unique words), unique (substantive content not on hub), mixed (some unique + significant redirection), redirect (pass-through). Refined by agents in Phase 3.

nodes.label: pages.jsonl Record Schema
nodes.description: Per-page record: url, normalizedUrl, unitName, unitLevel, faculty, subdomain, domain, section, breadcrumb[], title, depth, navItems[], wordCountUnique, outboundLinks[{text,href}], destinationsSummary{host:count}, classification, topicTags[], rawTextFile, capturedAt.

nodes.label: Academic Unit Registry
nodes.description: CSV registry of ~44 academic units (9 faculties + ~35 schools/departments/institutes/centres), each with unitName, unitLevel, faculty, site host/path, hasCSSection flag, and CS entry URL. Built by probing each unit's site for a Current Students section.

nodes.label: My Course Planner
nodes.description: Central university tool for course planning, linked from multiple faculty sites. Hub has the tool but no degree-specific guides — the gap it tries to fill.

nodes.label: MyTimetable
nodes.description: Central university timetabling system. Hub-owned but referenced by faculty sites for subject scheduling and class registration.

nodes.label: Squiz Matrix CMS
nodes.description: The university's web CMS used by Law, Arts, FEIT, and Science with consistent global header/footer. Other faculties (FFAM, MDHS, FBE, Biomedical Sciences) use different templates or treatments — contributing to inconsistent student experience.

nodes.label: Stop 1 (Student Services)
nodes.description: Central student services hub on students.unimelb.edu.au — the primary contact point for enrolment, fees, exams, graduation, support services. The definitive transactional administration front door.

nodes.label: scholarships.unimelb.edu.au
nodes.description: Central scholarships catalogue and application portal. Currently holds zero faculty scholarship content despite being the natural consolidation point. Recommendation target for migrating faculty scholarship pages.

nodes.label: LANTITE (Literacy and Numeracy Test for Initial Teacher Education)
nodes.description: A regulatory requirement for Education students. Faculty-specific content on the Education CS section — no hub equivalent. Example of program-specific regulatory content that cannot be centralized.

nodes.label: Fitness to Practice
nodes.description: MDHS requirement ensuring students meet professional standards for clinical practice. Faculty must own this content — regulatory, discipline-specific, cannot be centralized.

nodes.label: Duplication Heatmap
nodes.description: Cross-service analysis ranking duplication severity: careers (🔴 High — 8/9 faculties), course planning (🔴 High — 9/9 + schools, but mostly legitimate), student life (🟡 Medium), academic skills (🟡 Medium), scholarships (🟡 Medium), health & wellbeing (🟡 Medium), enrolment (🟢 Low), exams (🟢 Low), placements (🟢 Low — legitimate).

nodes.label: Page Depth & Complexity Analysis
nodes.description: The four fully-crawled faculties (Law, FEIT, Arts, Science) account for 75% of all pages. Law and FEIT alone account for 51%. Total crawl: ~780 pages, ~650K words across 9 faculties + 3 schools + hub. Law: 195 pages/150K words, FEIT: 204/156K, Arts: 91/80K, Science: 94/90K.

nodes.label: Hub is Definitive for Transactional Admin
nodes.description: Enrolment, fees, key dates, graduation, special consideration, results — the hub is the single source of truth. Faculties that provide parallel content (FEIT, Law, Arts) are duplicating unnecessarily.

nodes.label: MDHS is the Most Fragmented Faculty
nodes.description: Content scattered across faculty hub + 3+ school subdomains with different URL patterns. A Biomedicine student potentially navigates 3 different CS experiences (hub, MDHS faculty, Biomedical Sciences school).

nodes.label: Every Faculty Runs Own CS Section
nodes.description: All 9 faculties run their own local Current Students section — none rely solely on the central hub. But at four different URL conventions, an early consistency red flag. Schools/departments/institutes/centres (0 of ~33) do not run their own CS sections — except for the 3 MDHS schools + MBS.

nodes.label: Alice (Project Participant)
nodes.description: Participant in voice transcript discussion about the UniMelb CS IA project — contributed code blocks and project structure discussion.

nodes.label: Rey (Project Participant)
nodes.description: Participant in voice transcript discussion about content blocks and project methodology for the UniMelb CS IA project.

nodes.label: Project Execution Plan
nodes.description: Four-phase plan: Phase 0 (setup), Phase 1 (unit registry + CS probe + seed frontier), Phase 2 (exhaustive resumable crawl via Claude-in-Chrome), Phase 3 (multi-agent analysis workflow reading local corpus), Phase 4 (recommendations synthesis + branded PDF + executive deck). Resumable via state/frontier.json + visited.json + progress.md.

nodes.label: Genuine Hub Content Gaps (8 Items)
nodes.description: Services existing only on faculty sites with no hub equivalent: (1) Course plans/sample plans, (2) Placement/practicum management, (3) Discipline-specific academic skills, (4) Faculty forms, (5) Program-specific regulatory requirements (LANTITE, Fitness to Practice), (6) Faculty-specific enrichment, (7) Performance/practice management (FFAM), (8) School-level course planning (BBiomed, MBS).

nodes.label: Inconsistent Sub-Unit Routing
nodes.description: Science and FEIT schools route to faculty CS; Arts schools route to faculty CS with some deep-linking; MDHS schools route to central hub, NOT faculty CS — so MDHS students get a different 'home' depending on whether they land on the faculty site vs a school site; FBE departments route to central hub.

nodes.label: CS Section 'Shape' Variation
nodes.description: Faculty CS sections have fundamentally different structures: Science uses single landing page with in-page anchors; Law uses true sub-tree (/students/jd, /students/masters, /students/graduate-research); Education uses task pages; MDHS uses fitness-to-practice + placements + student hub cards. No consistent IA model.

nodes.label: Indigenous Student Support
nodes.description: Hub has Indigenous students section. Wilin Centre (FFAM) additionally keeps its own Indigenous student-support page. FEIT has Indigenous Student Experience page. Limited faculty coverage overall.

nodes.label: Study Overseas & Exchange
nodes.description: Faculty-owned outbound mobility content: degree partnerships (Law), exchange partners, international internships (FEIT), global challenges, travelling studios (MSD). Hub has study overseas section but much faculty detail is unique — Law alone has 55 exchange pages.

hyperedges.description: Careers & employability is duplicated by 8/9 faculties in parallel with the central hub. The recommendation is NOT to migrate all career content to the hub (faculty-specific mentoring, internships, and career pathways are legitimate), but rather to add bidirectional cross-links so students using either system discover the other.

The clearest consolidation target: enrolment, fees, exams, graduation, and special consideration are already definitively hub-owned. Faculty restatements of these services (especially by FEIT, Law, Arts) should be replaced with links to the hub. Only 6 of 227 transactional-admin pages sit on the hub domain — the rest is faculty noise.

MDHS is the most fragmented faculty: content scattered across faculty hub + 3+ school subdomains with different URL patterns. Schools route to the central hub, NOT the faculty CS — so a Biomedicine student potentially navigates 3 different CS experiences. Contrasts with all other faculties where schools route through the faculty CS page.

Four classifier artifacts systematically inflate topic counts: (1) MSD ~700 near-identical studio pages → subjects-timetable & student-life; (2) FEIT path-nesting under /orientation/ → orientation & it-systems; (3) ~70 Law researcher bios → research-candidature & inclusion-equity; (4) tag mislabelling: international is mostly outbound mobility, graduation is mostly HDR lifecycle. Fixes: de-archive studios, flatten FEIT URLs, convert bios to directory, fix Biomedical redirect stubs.

Several content areas are genuinely faculty-owned and should NOT be centralized: degree-specific course plans, placements/WIL (clinical, teaching, legal, engineering), discipline-specific academic skills (legal research, lab skills, studio practice), faculty-specific forms, research candidature milestones, and program-specific regulatory requirements (LANTITE, Fitness to Practice). These are operational requirements of each discipline.

Multiple findings converge on the need for IA standardization: four URL conventions make CS sections undiscoverable, URL traps cause 404s, CMS inconsistency fragments the visual experience, CS section shapes vary dramatically, and no page tells students 'what's where.' The recommendation bundle: standardize URL pattern + IA template + add a 'what's where' service map.

The crawl is constrained: WebFetch is WAF-blocked (403), Apify over monthly limit, so all fetching is via Claude-in-Chrome — sequential, resumable across sessions. Pages are BFS-crawled per unit, classified (link-farm/unique/mixed/redirect), and stored in pages.jsonl with raw text. The analysis phase reads the static corpus, never the live site.
